It is also called ``Senda Shrine'' or ``Osenda-san'' after the name of the place where it is enshrined. It is located halfway up Mt. Nakao, a hill in the southern part of Senda, Arita City, facing south. === Enshrined God === [[File:Kuniteru Gozu dragon.jpg|thumb|150px|right|[[Susanoo|Susanoo]]]] The following deities are enshrined.The enshrined deities are listed in the shrine's history book. . * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' (Susanoo no Mikoto) === About the enshrined deity === As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''''. In ancient times, in {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} first year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) appears to be the divine name of ``Susa no Kami'', but ``Susanoo'' is thought to mean ``Susa man'' after the place name ``Susa''. .In "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}", in the section about visiting "{{ill|lt=Kennokenshukuni|UNKNOWN|lt=Kennokenshukuni}}", {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q27 6944|WD=Q27 6944|lt=It is written that when Ōanamu Sakugami}} (Ōkuninushi) headed to ``Ne no Kenshu Province, where Susano no Mikoto is seated,'' he first aimed for ``Kuni no Kuni'' (the ancient name of Kii Province). In other words, Kii is envisioned as the entrance to Nekenshu Province (Ne no Kuni), and there is a relationship between ``Susanoo no Mikoto'' who sits in Ne no Kuni and Kii's ``Susa God'' (our company's enshrined deity) at the entrance.In "Kojiki," it is {{ill|lt=Oyabikojin|Q10935046|WD=Q10935046|1=Oyabiko}} of the Boku no Kuni region who sent Oanamutsokugami to Susanoo, the land of roots, but this god is said to be the same god as Susanoo's child god, Isotake no Kami. . {{ill|lt=Ken Matsumae|Q11529297|WD=Q11529297|1=Takeshi Matsumae}}, looking at this together with our company's sea god-like nature, speculates that our company is the origin of the Susanoo faith that spreads all over the country, and that the original deity is the one who visits at set times to bring fertility from the land of roots, which is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=eternal country|1=UNKNOWN}} across the sea. (See later section "{{ill|lt=#|UNKNOWN|lt=Appendix}}").
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'''
The names of the gods are written in the Nihon Shoki, and the names of the shrines are written in the Engishiki. {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01='''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}'''
('''Susa Shrine''')}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|-|.| }} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01={{ill|lt=50 Fierce God|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=50 Fierce Life|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
({{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}})|02={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|1=Oyatsuhime Shrine}})|03={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}})}}{{familytree/end}}
In addition, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the Jindai 1st Stage), Susanoo's {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} is described as {{jalink|Fifty Fierce Gods|Q10881556|Fifty Taken no Mikoto} } (Isotakeru/Itakeru), {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} (Ooyatsuhime), {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} (Tsumatsuhime) are described. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought wood seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii, they are collectively known as the ``Ita-ke-so Sanjin'', and each is located at (Kiso), {{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556|1=Oyatsuhime Shrine}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} (3 Ronsha shrines). Our company has a particularly deep relationship with Itakeso Shrine, and our (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe}} is said to have been located adjacent to Itaigeso Shrine (see the later section "{{ill|lt=#History|UNKNOWN|lt=History}}"), and it is said that the shrine's members visited during festivals, and historical sources from {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}{{ill|lt=Kaneki Yoshida|Q723266|WD=Q723266|1=Yoshida Kanetomo}} "Shinmeicho headnote". In , you can also see the description that ``It is a subsidiary shrine of Iso Taijin Mama.''Since ancient times, our company has been revered by {{ill|lt=Amajin|Q1577970|WD=Q1577970|1=Ama (diving)}} of the Kii region, who are involved in fishing, hunting, and navigation as gods of fishing and trees that supply trees for ships. Also, according to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}", in the early modern period, he was worshiped as a god that would protect against sword damage, and there was an ancient tradition of awarding divine talismans to protect against sword damage. === History == = === Founding === [[File:Itakisojinja 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{ill|lt=Itakisojinja Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}Itakisojinja). The enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} is said to be the son god of Susanoo. It has a deep relationship with Susa Shrine since ancient times. ]]According to the shrine biography cited in "Zoku Fudoki", in {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=713|Q23523|WD=Q23523|1=713}}), in the beginning of October {{ill|lt=Rai|Q877261|WD=Q877261|lt=Rai's day|1=Pig (zodiac)}}. |Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}} A shrine called Soshu was worshiped from Nishikawamine (location unknown) to the summit of Mt. Nakaoyama in 1985. In addition, in the same story, it was originally facing west facing the sea, but because it exhibited supernatural powers such as capsizing ships that did not show respect, It is said that by an imperial order in 715 | Q23528}}, it was moved to its current location halfway up the hillside and oriented south so that the sea could not be seen. The first day of the Pig in October of the 6th year of Wado, when our company is said to have been solicited, coincides with the date when it is said that {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location ({{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}Itaikiso}). "Zoku Fudoki" points out the relationship between the two shrines based on this {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}.It is said that there has been a long-established faith in the Kii region, as seen in the tale of Kenshu Province, which is the root of the Kojiki . During that period, around the middle to late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (5th and 6th centuries), diplomatic relations with Korea were based on Kii's {{ill|lt=gozoku|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} and Ki clan ({{jalink|Kii {{ill|lt=Toshio Kishi|Q11475787|WD=Q11475787|1=Toshio Kishi}} "A Study on the Ki clan" (Research on Ancient Japanese Political History, published by Hanawa Shobo, 1966) included). There is also a view that the company was already enshrined by the Ama people led by Mr. Nori.The site of Susa Shrine belongs to the ancient {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Arita District|Q1131181|WD=Q1131181|lt=Arida District|1=Arida District, Wakayama}} (Arita District) Susago. The place name ``Susa'' comes from the song ``{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}'', ``The Arisomatsu of the cove of Susa, where Aji lives, the children who wait for me are all alone.'' Manyoshu, Volume 11, No. 2751. See [http://infux03.inf.edu.yamaguchi-u.ac.jp/~manyou/ver2_2/manyou_kekka2.php?kekka=11/2751 11/2751] (Yamaguchi University "Manyoshu Search System"). ``Aji'' is the old name for {{ill|lt=Tomoe duck|Q27461190|WD=Q27461190|1=Baikal teal}} (Ajikamo), and ``Araiso pine'' refers to a pine tree that grows on a rocky shore where rough waves are approaching. There is a theory that it is the same as the "Nagisa" seen in {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)|1985}}However, this "Nagisha" is referred to in "Man'yoshu" as "The place where Aji lives, the Komorinu swamp in the inlet of Susa, breathing in Mizuku" (Volume 14) 3547) Similarly, there is also a theory that it refers to Susa Bay (currently Toyohama Port) in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Chita County|Q767312|WD=Q767312|1=Chita District}}{{ill|lt=Minamichita Town|Q1203589|WD=Q1203589|1=Minamichita}} Toyohama near the tip of {{ill|lt=Chita Peninsula|Q1074888|WD=Q1074888|1=Chita Peninsula}} (Yoshio Matsuda's Manyo Research Niimi and Demonstration'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okukaedesha|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1968). . In addition, at the northern end of Mt. Nakao in this area, there is a {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tumulus Group|Q42581362|WD=Q42581362|1=Okazaki Tumulus Group}} dating back to the 6th and 7th centuries, which has been archaeologically linked to our company {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987|p=101-103}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ====First seen in the literature is "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) Published in 806. In , 10 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Susa no Mikoto'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei|1=Jōhei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789|1=931}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794|1=938}}) In "{{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}" Kii "Susa-Kobe" can be seen in the country {{ill|lt=Nagasa-gun|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}}, but this is said to be the meaning of our company's Kobe, and it is said that it was established in Nagusa-gun due to the small size of Arita-gun {{Sfn|Susa-Kobe (Taira)|1983}}. This Kobe is identified as the area around Kuchi-Susa and Oku-Susa in Wakayama City, which borders Ita-Kiso Shrine.{{Sfn|Susa-Kobe (Taira)|1983}}According to the ``Kanei-ki'' included in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' and ``Ki Tsuzuki Fudoki,'' this Susa-Kobe existed until the early modern period. . Susa Shrine, the former Mukakusha, once existed in Kuchisusa (Kuchisusa Kamienmanji Temple, {{Coord|34|11|55.8|N|135|14|38.3|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Susa Shrine Ruins (Susa Kobe Estimated Site)}}). According to "Zoku Fudoki", this Susa Shrine was the one that invited our company to Susa-Kobe, but in July 1942 ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), it was enshrined at Gion Shrine, a terminal shrine within the precincts of Ita-Kiso Shrine.As a {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, he was promoted to the upper rank of Jugoi in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. Also {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} has "Susa Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} as ``Monthly Niiname'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninamesai|1=UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was kept in {{ill|lt=copybook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. In "{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}", the Tenjin is listed as ``Juichii Susa Okami'' {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}.{{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei|1=Jōhei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789|1=931}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794|1=938}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|1=UNKNOWN}}'', as mentioned above, ``Susa-Kobe'' can be seen in Nagusa District, Kii Province, and ``Susago'' can be seen in Zaida District, Kii Province, and these are place names related to our company {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)|1985}}. ==== After the Middle Ages ==== In the shrine biography cited in "Zoku Fudoki", there are 2{{ill|lt=town (unit)|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=town|1=chō}} (approximately 2{{ill|lt=ha|Q35852|WD=Q35852|1=Hectare}}) in Yasuda Shonai, where our company belongs, and {{ill|lt=Hidaka County (Wakayama Prefecture)|Q1131164|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District|1=Hidaka District, Wakayama}} It is said that more than 30 towns (approximately 36 hectares) in Tomiyasu Shonai were shared with Itaikeso ShrineHowever, according to the old document in the collection of Ita-ke-so Shrine ("I-ta-ke-so shrine Hisayasu document"), as of October 1148, the company owned two towns of Hota-no-sho and more than 30 towns of Toya-no-no-no-no-cho ({{jalin Although it is recognized that he owned Kagura rice fields (the rice fields used for {{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|1=Kagura}} offerings in rituals) such as the northern part of Gobo City|Q847572}}, there is no mention of the shared relationship with Susa Shrine. Regarding the Hisayasu document at Ita-keiso Shrine, it is cited in later historical materials, not from the time of Hisayasu, so there is a theory that it is a quote from a document from that time ({{ill|lt=Rizo Takeuchi|Q4262779|WD=Q4262779|1=Rizō Takeuchi}} ed. "Heian Ibun" Classical Documents Volume 11 "Commentary", {{ill|lt=Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1971), there is a theory that there is still room for consideration as to its authenticity ("Wakayama Prefectural History" Ancient Historical Materials 2, Wakayama Prefecture, 1989). , {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the faith of samurai families was added to this.According to the company biography, {{ill|lt=Kenmu (Japan)|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} In May 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}}), {{ill|lt=Masashige Kusunoki|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} donated the entire area of Yasuda (same as Yasudasho above) as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=company territory|1=UNKNOWN}}. , in August of the 2nd year of Kenmu, it was designated by {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} as the place of imperial request of Emperor Genmei and Emperor Godaigo, and it was decorated with plaques of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinji|1=UNKNOWN}} and soldiers ({{jalin {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=101-103}}, where four weapons (such as k|sword|Q7523706}} and {{ill|lt=戟|Q1368004|WD=Q1368004|1=Ji (polearm)}}) were dedicated. However, in {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s attack on Yuasa in March 7th year ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}), However, Hideyoshi's attack on Yuasa in 1999 is nowhere to be seen (see "{{ill|lt=Kishu Conquest#Nobunaga's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}"). , the company is said to have been destroyed by Shirakashizaemon Jojitsufusa of {{ill|lt=Yuasa-cho|Q1345625|WD=Q1345625|lt=Yuasa|1=Yuasa, Wakayama}}, who responded privately to Hideyoshi. At that time, the {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}, Shigemasa Oe, stored sacred treasures and old records in a {{ill|lt=kara chest|Q11418586|WD=Q11418586|1=kara chest}} in the forest of Kokodani behind the shrine. However, it is said that all of the above-mentioned shrine treasures and old records were lost as they were searched by Sanefusa and burned or dumped into the sea.After that, in {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|WD=Q6725|1=1601}}), the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Kii}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lord of the domain|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Yukinaga Asano|Q1143577|WD=Q1143577|1=Asano Yoshinaga}} {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080|1=Shinden (divine field)}}14{{ill|lt=ding|Q1078373|WD=Q1078373|1=Hinoto (丁)}}3{{ill|lt=anti|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|1=tan}} (14 hectares) was donated, and the shrine building was rebuilt. {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) on behalf of Mr. Asano The first lord of the {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|WD=Q11605046|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, {{ill|lt=Yorinobu Tokugawa|Q1045763|WD=Q1045763|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, once again established the shrine territory 5 {{ill|lt=stone (Unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=Stone|1=Koku}} was donated, and it was stipulated that the construction and repair of the shrine building would be borne by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kii clan|1=UNKNOWN}} with the domain expenses.{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)| In 1985}}{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}, it was deeply revered by successive lords of the Kishu Tokugawa domain throughout the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), {{jalink|Modern company rank system 22 shrines listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrines|1=UNKNOWN}} in |Q712534}} in Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}) (all {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}) The breakdown is as follows (``Arita Gunshi''). * Senda 6 shrines - Hachiman Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Konpira Shrine, Myoken Shrine, Omiki Shrine * Tsujido 4 shrines - Tai Shrine, Konpira Shrine, Benten Shrine, Gion Shrine * Hoshio 2 shrines - Inari Shrine, Mizu Shrine * Yamadahara 5 Shrines - Tenjin Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Hachioji Shrine, Myoken Shrine, Inari Shrine * Shimonakajima 5 shrines - Inari Shrine, Kifune Shrine, Gion Shrine, Toyokane Shrine, Akiba Shrine The following year, it was enshrined at Hoshio Ichinomiya Shrine (currently Gokasho Shrine, a precinct shrine). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} First year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) New Year 27th, from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Susagami.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Juichii ("{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}") - Written as "Susa Okami." === Shinto priest === Since the Middle Ages, our company's {{ill|lt=Shake|Q11588367|WD=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} has been held by the Oga clan (in olden times, they used the surnames Oe and Iwahashi). === Precincts === [[File:Susa-jinja (Arida) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Full view of the temple grounds. The approach is from left to right at the bottom of the image. On the upper right are the main shrine, oryoden, worship hall, and bell gate. ]]``The main hall'' is made of Ikkensha Sumikiri {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} and has a copper plate roof. The front pillar space is 1 ken, but the back and sides are 2 ken, and corner blocks are placed on the front pillars, which is a structure that follows the Kumano-zukuri style. . This was done by order of the feudal lord, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|Q319664|WD=Q319664|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}. (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} It was rebuilt in September of the first year ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}), and the Nakamura family, the head carpenters of the Kishu clan, was involved in its construction, and it appears that it was directly managed by the clan."Arita Regional Cultural Properties Catalog", Arita Regional Cultural Properties Protection Deliberation Committee Liaison Council, 1997. . On three sides of the front side of the main building, there is a rim with a raised railing (large floor), and side shoji screens are installed at the rear of both sides. The interior is divided into the {{ill|lt=inner hall|Q14616641|WD=Q14616641|1=Presbytery (architecture)}} and the outer hall by installing front and rear partitions on the central column of the main building, and a board door with a scroll, but it is said to be unusual in that the outer hall floor is one step higher than the kirikina nagaoshi and lower than the edge. Although there are few decorations such as carvings and the paintings are modest, it has a dignified appearance and is a valuable relic that shows the style of the Nakamura family . Originally it was {{ill|lt=cypress bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, but in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696} }), the roof was replaced with copper plates, and extensive repairs were carried out from {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}}) to 9. This main shrine has been designated as a cultural property designated by Arita City.In front of the main shrine are the ``Chumon Gate'' and the ``Oratory Hall'', which are connected to the ``Oratory Hall''. The middle gate is a flat {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Karamon|1=UNKNOWN}}, the hall of worship is a flat {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with 3 girders and 2 beams, and the worship hall is a gabled tsumairi with 1 beam and 3 girders, and both have copper plate roofs. The hall of worship and the hall of worship are both open-air. The hall of worship is lower than the hall of worship, and the hall of worship has an upper floor in the center. The ``Suzumon'' (four-legged gate) standing in front of the shrine is {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} flat and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=tiled roof|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition, there are shrine buildings such as Kama-dono, {{ill|lt=Kagura-den|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}, and Ema-do hall in the precincts. Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukiyomison|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amateur Rikiojin|UNKNOWN|lt=Terikiojin}}. * West Hall (Izanami Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}}. * Gokasho Shrine and Inari Shrine (2 shrines {{ill|lt=Aiden|Q135009883|WD=Q135009883|1=Aidono}}) - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kikunsuchichi no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} and 24 other shrines (Gokasho Shrine also enshrines the deities of the combined shrines at the end of the Meiji period). * Kanayama Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamabikojin|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Sumiyoshi Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Three Gods|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Omotesu Otoko no Mikoto, Nakazutsu Otoko no Mikoto, Sokotsutsu Otoko no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. * Itsukushima Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. * Shinkodani Shrine - Enshrined deity unknown. * Ita-Kiso Shrine Far Worship Hall * Minatogawa Shrine - Enshrined deity: Masashige Kusunoki. * Nanryusha - Enshrined deity: Yorinobu Tokugawa. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Oimono no Nushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomono no Nushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. * Koyasu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tanatsuhime no Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}. === Festival === === Annual festival === * Monthly Festival (1st day of every month) *Monthly Festival (14th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Congee fortune telling (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Lecture (February) * Spring Festival (Prayer Year Festival) (March) * Big catch festival (April) * Summer Purification (June) * Summer Festival Yoimiya (July 13th) * Summer Festival (July 14th) * Wakoshi (June 30th of the lunar calendar) * Senda Festival (October 14th) * Winter Festival (Ninamesai) (November) * Great purification ceremony (December 31st) === Main festivals === ; Senda Festival (regular festival) : {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}. Our {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} is called ``''Chida Festival''''. The annual festival used to be held on the first day of September {{ill|lt=rabbit|Q16662792|WD=Q16662792|lt=rabbit|1=mǎo}}. {{ill|lt=Masayasu|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}) Revised on September 14th by Yorinobu "Shrine Statement Book" in 1914. , later changed to {{ill|lt=solar calendar|Q210953|WD=Q210953|1=Solar calendar}} and became the current date.: The day before the festival (13th) is Yoimiya. The next day, on the 14th, after the Shinto ritual, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=haload|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=manager|Q7400093|WD=Q7400093|1=Saihai}} were performed in front of the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. alink|Forage|Q11498259}} A {{ill|lt=Lion Dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} consisting of {{ill|lt=Tengu|Q742360|WD=Q742360|1=Tengu}} and {{ill|lt=Oni|Q730052|WD=Q730052|1=Oni}} was dedicated. group="Note">The lion dance was previously held every other year by the two districts of Senda and Takada, but now it is held jointly ([http://bunkashisan.ne.jp/search/ViewContent.php?from=11&ContentID=241 Regional Cultural Asset Portal, Susa Shrine Lion Dance] Next, the mikoshi is carried to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho|1=UNKNOWN}} located to the southwest of the shrine.The mikoshi is thrown to the ground during the process.: A lion dance and other performances are held at the temple, and in the evening, a ``Tainage Shinji'' ceremony is held in which 6 {{ill|lt=tails#trivia|Q60960|WD=Q60960|lt=tails|1=Tail}} of sea bream are offered in front of the shrine and fishermen who have gathered compete for them. It is said that it was once so popular that people would say, ``If you want to see a bride, then it's the Senda Festival'' {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Corner)|1985}}, and even today it is also called the ``Senda Fighting Festival'' because fishermen fiercely compete for the sea bream thrown at them, believing that whoever gets it will be blessed with a big catch for a year. Afterwards, at midnight, they return to {{ill|lt=main shrine|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and the ritual ends. The festival mentioned above has been designated as a cultural property of Arita City as the ``Senda Festival.'' ; Congee fortune-telling ritual : {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}. {{ill|lt=Azuki bean porridge|Q115036294|WD=Q115036294|1=Azuki bean porridge}} is cooked in the cauldron in the temple grounds, and {{ill|lt=Glue fortune-telling|Q11604655|WD=Q11604655|1=Kayu ura}} is held to choose the best rice variety for the year from among 10 varieties. Afterwards, we eat red bean porridge and pray for good health throughout the year. According to ``Zoku Fudoki,'' until the Tensho period (the end of the 16th century), 12 sacred horses were dedicated from Itageso Shrine at the Senda Festival, and priests from the company participated in the ritual, and 12 priests from the company also visited the shrine every year on the first day of the New Year. It is thought that these were rituals in which the company's enshrined deity (Isotake no Mikoto) had an audience with his father deity {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Gods; First Edition)|1986|p=350-352}}. === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * Tachi inscription: Inshu Jukagecho 1 mouth (with Itomaki Tachi Koshirae)*: It was dedicated in 1721, 50 years after the death of the founder of the Kii Tokugawa clan, {{ill|lt=Yorinobu|Q1045763|WD=Q1045763|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, by Yoshimune Tokugawa, Yorinobu's grandson and the eighth shogun. It was created by Kagenaga, a {{ill|lt=swordsmith|Q91012922|WD=Q91012922|1=Japanese swordsmith}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inshu|1=UNKNOWN}} (currently {{ill|lt=Tottori Prefecture|Q133935|WD=Q133935|1=Tottori Prefecture}}) during the Muromachi period. Blade length: 68.2cm, 1.8cm long, Kazukuri-an-bu. The figure of the sword is slender, the base iron is koitame, and the blade pattern is a thin straight blade (hoso-suguha), which is typical of Yamashiroden. ``Wakayama Prefecture Cultural Properties Guidebook'', Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education, 1999. . The Tachi Koshirae was made in the Edo period, and the thread wrapping and metal fittings are decorated with a warihishimon crest painted in shakudo iroe, and the {{ill|lt=sheath|Q661140|WD=Q661140|1=Scabbard}} is decorated with a warihishimon crest painted in {{ill|lt=maki-e|Q2083390|WD=Q2083390|1=Maki-e}}. {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}}) On March 26, 1915, National Treasure (so-called {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old National Treasure|1=UNKNOWN}}) It was designated as a {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} with the enactment of {{ill|lt=Cultural Properties Protection Law|Q1188502|WD=Q1188502|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law}} on August 29, 1950 ({{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|WD=Q18597|1=1950}}). === Cultural property designated by Arita City === * Main shrine * Tachi Mei Keishin 1 mouth (included Itomaki Tachi Koshirae) *: A sword made by Keishin Osafune, a resident of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}} (currently {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}) during the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. A tachi with a blade length of 63.6 cm and a length of 2.2 cm. The blade pattern is clove. Dedicated by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Munenao|Q6993442|WD=Q6993442|1=Tokugawa Munenao}}. The Koshirae was made in the Edo period, and both the {{ill|lt=handle|Q19508474|WD=Q19508474|1=grip}} and sheath are made of purple thread. The metal fittings show {{ill|lt=diamond|Q11620223|WD=Q11620223|1=Hishi mon}} painted in red bronze.The scabbard is decorated with {{ill|lt=Maki-e|Q2083390|WD=Q2083390|1=Maki-e}}, and the metal fittings and the Aoi crest (Aoi crest|Q11353976}}) are arranged alternately. * Senda Festival === Others === * Nanki Shigekuni dedication sword *: Although unspecified, this long sword has the inscription ``The petitioner is Kishu Jumonjusukan Sukejukuni 27 years old'' and has the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=god name|1=UNKNOWN}} of ``Susa Daimyojin'' engraved on it. The base and blade are thickly covered with rust, but {{jalink|Nanki Shigekuni #4th Generation It is known that it was dedicated on September 14, {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1690|Q7678|WD=Q7678|1=1690}}) by Shigekuni|Q11408354|4th Nanki Shigekuni}}, and from this dedication date and inscription, Shigekuni's year of birth can also be determined . * Motoori Norinaga poem monument *: In November of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=1794|Q7811|WD=Q7811|1=1794}}), {{ill|lt=Norinaga Motoori|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} read the {{ill|lt=adopted child|Q11572068|WD=Q11572068|1=yūshi}} At the invitation of the {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}} Iwahashi Tokizaki, he and I Ohira|Q3325272|Ohira}} stayed at the house of the priest, and preached {{ill|lt=old studies|Q9565755|WD=Q9565755|1=kogaku}} and {{ill|lt=poetry|Q11545302|WD=Q11545302|1=kadō}}. The monument was erected in November 1979 to commemorate his visit to our company on that occasion and the poem he wrote, ``This is the shrine of the gods of Iketaru, the kingdom of time, and the shrine of the gods of Fukaburo.'' Standing in front of the third torii gate. === Historical evidence === {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%; text-align:left" align="right" |+Similar internal company names of Kii and Izumo !God's name !{{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Kii Province|Q11604948|WD=Q11604948|lt=Kii|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province}} !{{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Izumo country|Q11395853|WD=Q11395853|lt=Izumo|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province}} |- !Itate | Date Shrine| />Sokinnoya Shrine Korea Itai Shrine |- !Kata |Kata Shrine||Kata Shrine |- !kumano ||Kumanoza Shrine||Kumanoza Shrine |- !Susa |Susa Shrine||Susa Shrine |- !Hayatama |Kumano Hayatama Shrine | | Hayatama Shrine |} [[File:Susa Shrine Hall of Worship.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[Sosa Shrine (Izumo City)|Susa Shrine]] ({{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}})]]Not only Susa Shrine, there are many shrines in {{ill|lt=Izumo|Q11395836|WD=Q11395836|1=Izumo}} and Kii that have the same company name (see the table on the right). Regarding this, there is a theory that both originated in Izumo and that the Izumo people brought their home country's religion with them when they settled in Kii. , there is a theory by Ken Matsumae and others that considers Kii to be the original homeland. Regarding Susanoo, it has been pointed out from an early stage that there is a discrepancy between the inner nature of Susanoo as a god of destruction and tyranny as seen in the Kiki and the inner nature as {{ill|lt=Bunka Hero|Q977690|WD=Q977690|lt=Bunka Hero God|1=Culture hero}} as seen in ``{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', and various theories have been proposed. .According to Ken Matsumae's theory, if Susanoo, which appears in both the Kojiki, Nihon Shoki, and Fudoki, is viewed as a separate entity, the deep kinship with the ocean-like and tree-like divinity and the land of roots seen in the Kojiki cannot be found in the Fudoki, but it can be seen in the Kii Company. From this, we infer that the Susanoo mentioned in the ``Kojiki'' and ``Nihonshoki'' refers to the deity enshrined at our company. He also cites the theory that the Land of Nenes, an ``eternal country'' in the Kii region where Susano-o is said to visit, was equated in the central government with Nekuni, which is the ``source of disaster'' and ``the land that sends back disasters,'' and that Susano-o was also seen as a god of destruction and tyranny that brought disaster. Furthermore, as supporting evidence that Susanoo from the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki is the deity enshrined by our company, we cite the importance placed on it by the Imperial Court, such as the designation of the shrine as a famous deity and records of investitures . Regarding the relationship with Izumo, Ken Matsumae also states that some of the Kaijin of Kii, who were honored with Susanoo and engaged in diplomacy with the continent (Korea), settled in Izumo. It is assumed that as the faith in Susanoo spread to the inland areas of the Izumo region, the inner nature of the ocean god etc. was lost. Another theory is that the complexity of Susanoo's divinity arose as a result of the thoughtless reunification of the Susanoo statues that existed in the central aristocracy and the Izumo region at the time of the compilation of the Kiki. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items It is also called ``Senda Shrine'' or ``Osenda-san'' after the name of the place where it is enshrined. It is located halfway up Mt. Nakao, a hill in the southern part of Senda, Arita City, facing south. === Enshrined God === [[File:Kuniteru Gozu dragon.jpg|thumb|150px|right|[[Susanoo|Susanoo]]]] The following deities are enshrined.The enshrined deities are listed in the shrine's history book. . * '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' (Susanoo no Mikoto) === About the enshrined deity === As the company name suggests, our company is said to be a shrine dedicated to '''{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''''. In ancient times, in {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} first year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) appears to be the divine name of ``Susa no Kami'', but ``Susanoo'' is thought to mean ``Susa man'' after the place name ``Susa''. .In "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}", in the section about visiting "{{ill|lt=Kennokenshukuni|UNKNOWN|lt=Kennokenshukuni}}", {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q27 6944|WD=Q27 6944|lt=It is written that when Ōanamu Sakugami}} (Ōkuninushi) headed to ``Ne no Kenshu Province, where Susano no Mikoto is seated,'' he first aimed for ``Kuni no Kuni'' (the ancient name of Kii Province). In other words, Kii is envisioned as the entrance to Nekenshu Province (Ne no Kuni), and there is a relationship between ``Susanoo no Mikoto'' who sits in Ne no Kuni and Kii's ``Susa God'' (our company's enshrined deity) at the entrance.In "Kojiki," it is {{ill|lt=Oyabikojin|Q10935046|WD=Q10935046|1=Oyabiko}} of the Boku no Kuni region who sent Oanamutsokugami to Susanoo, the land of roots, but this god is said to be the same god as Susanoo's child god, Isotake no Kami. . {{ill|lt=Ken Matsumae|Q11529297|WD=Q11529297|1=Takeshi Matsumae}}, looking at this together with our company's sea god-like nature, speculates that our company is the origin of the Susanoo faith that spreads all over the country, and that the original deity is the one who visits at set times to bring fertility from the land of roots, which is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=eternal country|1=UNKNOWN}} across the sea. (See later section "{{ill|lt=#|UNKNOWN|lt=Appendix}}").

('''Susa Shrine''')}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|-|.| }} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01={{ill|lt=50 Fierce God|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=50 Fierce Life|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
({{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}})|02={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|1=Oyatsuhime Shrine}})|03={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{ill|lt=Tsumatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}})}}{{familytree/end}}
In addition, in the 5th book of the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' Jindaiki's Treasure Sword Appearance Stage (8th Stage of the Jindai 1st Stage), Susanoo's {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} is described as {{jalink|Fifty Fierce Gods|Q10881556|Fifty Taken no Mikoto} } (Isotakeru/Itakeru), {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime no Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} (Ooyatsuhime), {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Sakutsuhime no Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} (Tsumatsuhime) are described. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought wood seeds to Kii Province, and in Kii, they are collectively known as the ``Ita-ke-so Sanjin'', and each is located at (Kiso), {{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556|1=Oyatsuhime Shrine}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), and {{ill|lt=Tomatsuhime Shrine|Q17216259|WD=Q17216259|1=Tsumatsuhime Shrine}} (3 Ronsha shrines). Our company has a particularly deep relationship with Itakeso Shrine, and our (Private)|Q11589711|Kobe}} is said to have been located adjacent to Itaigeso Shrine (see the later section "{{ill|lt=#History|UNKNOWN|lt=History}}"), and it is said that the shrine's members visited during festivals, and historical sources from {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}{{ill|lt=Kaneki Yoshida|Q723266|WD=Q723266|1=Yoshida Kanetomo}} "Shinmeicho headnote". In , you can also see the description that ``It is a subsidiary shrine of Iso Taijin Mama.''Since ancient times, our company has been revered by {{ill|lt=Amajin|Q1577970|WD=Q1577970|1=Ama (diving)}} of the Kii region, who are involved in fishing, hunting, and navigation as gods of fishing and trees that supply trees for ships. Also, according to "{{ill|lt=Ki Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}", in the early modern period, he was worshiped as a god that would protect against sword damage, and there was an ancient tradition of awarding divine talismans to protect against sword damage. === History == = === Founding === [[File:Itakisojinja 02.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{ill|lt=Itakisojinja Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}Itakisojinja). The enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} is said to be the son god of Susanoo. It has a deep relationship with Susa Shrine since ancient times. ]]According to the shrine biography cited in "Zoku Fudoki", in {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=713|Q23523|WD=Q23523|1=713}}), in the beginning of October {{ill|lt=Rai|Q877261|WD=Q877261|lt=Rai's day|1=Pig (zodiac)}}. |Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}} A shrine called Soshu was worshiped from Nishikawamine (location unknown) to the summit of Mt. Nakaoyama in 1985. In addition, in the same story, it was originally facing west facing the sea, but because it exhibited supernatural powers such as capsizing ships that did not show respect, It is said that by an imperial order in 715 | Q23528}}, it was moved to its current location halfway up the hillside and oriented south so that the sea could not be seen. The first day of the Pig in October of the 6th year of Wado, when our company is said to have been solicited, coincides with the date when it is said that {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=transferred|1=UNKNOWN}} to its current location ({{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}Itaikiso}). "Zoku Fudoki" points out the relationship between the two shrines based on this {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}.It is said that there has been a long-established faith in the Kii region, as seen in the tale of Kenshu Province, which is the root of the Kojiki . During that period, around the middle to late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (5th and 6th centuries), diplomatic relations with Korea were based on Kii's {{ill|lt=gozoku|Q84066|WD=Q84066|1=Gōzoku}} and Ki clan ({{jalink|Kii {{ill|lt=Toshio Kishi|Q11475787|WD=Q11475787|1=Toshio Kishi}} "A Study on the Ki clan" (Research on Ancient Japanese Political History, published by Hanawa Shobo, 1966) included). There is also a view that the company was already enshrined by the Ama people led by Mr. Nori.The site of Susa Shrine belongs to the ancient {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Arita District|Q1131181|WD=Q1131181|lt=Arida District|1=Arida District, Wakayama}} (Arita District) Susago. The place name ``Susa'' comes from the song ``{{ill|lt=Man'yoshu|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}}'', ``The Arisomatsu of the cove of Susa, where Aji lives, the children who wait for me are all alone.'' Manyoshu, Volume 11, No. 2751. See [http://infux03.inf.edu.yamaguchi-u.ac.jp/~manyou/ver2_2/manyou_kekka2.php?kekka=11/2751 11/2751] (Yamaguchi University "Manyoshu Search System"). ``Aji'' is the old name for {{ill|lt=Tomoe duck|Q27461190|WD=Q27461190|1=Baikal teal}} (Ajikamo), and ``Araiso pine'' refers to a pine tree that grows on a rocky shore where rough waves are approaching. There is a theory that it is the same as the "Nagisa" seen in {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)|1985}}However, this "Nagisha" is referred to in "Man'yoshu" as "The place where Aji lives, the Komorinu swamp in the inlet of Susa, breathing in Mizuku" (Volume 14) 3547) Similarly, there is also a theory that it refers to Susa Bay (currently Toyohama Port) in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Chita County|Q767312|WD=Q767312|1=Chita District}}{{ill|lt=Minamichita Town|Q1203589|WD=Q1203589|1=Minamichita}} Toyohama near the tip of {{ill|lt=Chita Peninsula|Q1074888|WD=Q1074888|1=Chita Peninsula}} (Yoshio Matsuda's Manyo Research Niimi and Demonstration'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okukaedesha|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1968). . In addition, at the northern end of Mt. Nakao in this area, there is a {{ill|lt=Okazaki Tumulus Group|Q42581362|WD=Q42581362|1=Okazaki Tumulus Group}} dating back to the 6th and 7th centuries, which has been archaeologically linked to our company {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987|p=101-103}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ====First seen in the literature is "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) Published in 806. In , 10 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} (private houses attached to the shrine) were granted to ``Susa no Mikoto'' in Kii Province {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei|1=Jōhei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789|1=931}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794|1=938}}) In "{{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}" Kii "Susa-Kobe" can be seen in the country {{ill|lt=Nagasa-gun|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}}, but this is said to be the meaning of our company's Kobe, and it is said that it was established in Nagusa-gun due to the small size of Arita-gun {{Sfn|Susa-Kobe (Taira)|1983}}. This Kobe is identified as the area around Kuchi-Susa and Oku-Susa in Wakayama City, which borders Ita-Kiso Shrine.{{Sfn|Susa-Kobe (Taira)|1983}}According to the ``Kanei-ki'' included in ``Ki Meisho Zue'' and ``Ki Tsuzuki Fudoki,'' this Susa-Kobe existed until the early modern period. . Susa Shrine, the former Mukakusha, once existed in Kuchisusa (Kuchisusa Kamienmanji Temple, {{Coord|34|11|55.8|N|135|14|38.3|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Susa Shrine Ruins (Susa Kobe Estimated Site)}}). According to "Zoku Fudoki", this Susa Shrine was the one that invited our company to Susa-Kobe, but in July 1942 ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), it was enshrined at Gion Shrine, a terminal shrine within the precincts of Ita-Kiso Shrine.As a {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, he was promoted to the upper rank of Jugoi in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. Also {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa County|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} has "Susa Shrine" Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} as ``Monthly Niiname'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninamesai|1=UNKNOWN}}, it is written that it was kept in {{ill|lt=copybook|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}. In "{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}", the Tenjin is listed as ``Juichii Susa Okami'' {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}.{{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei|1=Jōhei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789|1=931}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794|1=938}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|1=UNKNOWN}}'', as mentioned above, ``Susa-Kobe'' can be seen in Nagusa District, Kii Province, and ``Susago'' can be seen in Zaida District, Kii Province, and these are place names related to our company {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)|1985}}. ==== After the Middle Ages ==== In the shrine biography cited in "Zoku Fudoki", there are 2{{ill|lt=town (unit)|Q1185752|WD=Q1185752|lt=town|1=chō}} (approximately 2{{ill|lt=ha|Q35852|WD=Q35852|1=Hectare}}) in Yasuda Shonai, where our company belongs, and {{ill|lt=Hidaka County (Wakayama Prefecture)|Q1131164|WD=Q1131164|lt=Hidaka District|1=Hidaka District, Wakayama}} It is said that more than 30 towns (approximately 36 hectares) in Tomiyasu Shonai were shared with Itaikeso ShrineHowever, according to the old document in the collection of Ita-ke-so Shrine ("I-ta-ke-so shrine Hisayasu document"), as of October 1148, the company owned two towns of Hota-no-sho and more than 30 towns of Toya-no-no-no-no-cho ({{jalin Although it is recognized that he owned Kagura rice fields (the rice fields used for {{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260|1=Kagura}} offerings in rituals) such as the northern part of Gobo City|Q847572}}, there is no mention of the shared relationship with Susa Shrine. Regarding the Hisayasu document at Ita-keiso Shrine, it is cited in later historical materials, not from the time of Hisayasu, so there is a theory that it is a quote from a document from that time ({{ill|lt=Rizo Takeuchi|Q4262779|WD=Q4262779|1=Rizō Takeuchi}} ed. "Heian Ibun" Classical Documents Volume 11 "Commentary", {{ill|lt=Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1971), there is a theory that there is still room for consideration as to its authenticity ("Wakayama Prefectural History" Ancient Historical Materials 2, Wakayama Prefecture, 1989). , {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the faith of samurai families was added to this.According to the company biography, {{ill|lt=Kenmu (Japan)|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} In May 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1335|Q6011|WD=Q6011|1=1335}}), {{ill|lt=Masashige Kusunoki|Q382234|WD=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} donated the entire area of Yasuda (same as Yasudasho above) as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=company territory|1=UNKNOWN}}. , in August of the 2nd year of Kenmu, it was designated by {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} as the place of imperial request of Emperor Genmei and Emperor Godaigo, and it was decorated with plaques of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinji|1=UNKNOWN}} and soldiers ({{jalin {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=101-103}}, where four weapons (such as k|sword|Q7523706}} and {{ill|lt=戟|Q1368004|WD=Q1368004|1=Ji (polearm)}}) were dedicated. However, in {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s attack on Yuasa in March 7th year ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}), However, Hideyoshi's attack on Yuasa in 1999 is nowhere to be seen (see "{{ill|lt=Kishu Conquest#Nobunaga's Kishu Attack|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721|1=Battle of Kishuu}}"). , the company is said to have been destroyed by Shirakashizaemon Jojitsufusa of {{ill|lt=Yuasa-cho|Q1345625|WD=Q1345625|lt=Yuasa|1=Yuasa, Wakayama}}, who responded privately to Hideyoshi. At that time, the {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}, Shigemasa Oe, stored sacred treasures and old records in a {{ill|lt=kara chest|Q11418586|WD=Q11418586|1=kara chest}} in the forest of Kokodani behind the shrine. However, it is said that all of the above-mentioned shrine treasures and old records were lost as they were searched by Sanefusa and burned or dumped into the sea.After that, in {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|WD=Q6725|1=1601}}), the {{ill|lt=Kii clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Kii}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lord of the domain|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Yukinaga Asano|Q1143577|WD=Q1143577|1=Asano Yoshinaga}} {{ill|lt=Kanda|Q10855080|WD=Q10855080|1=Shinden (divine field)}}14{{ill|lt=ding|Q1078373|WD=Q1078373|1=Hinoto (丁)}}3{{ill|lt=anti|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|1=tan}} (14 hectares) was donated, and the shrine building was rebuilt. {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) on behalf of Mr. Asano The first lord of the {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|WD=Q11605046|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}}, {{ill|lt=Yorinobu Tokugawa|Q1045763|WD=Q1045763|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, once again established the shrine territory 5 {{ill|lt=stone (Unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=Stone|1=Koku}} was donated, and it was stipulated that the construction and repair of the shrine building would be borne by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kii clan|1=UNKNOWN}} with the domain expenses.{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (corner)| In 1985}}{{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Taira)|1983|p=511-512}}, it was deeply revered by successive lords of the Kishu Tokugawa domain throughout the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), {{jalink|Modern company rank system 22 shrines listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrines|1=UNKNOWN}} in |Q712534}} in Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}) (all {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}) The breakdown is as follows (``Arita Gunshi''). * Senda 6 shrines - Hachiman Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Konpira Shrine, Myoken Shrine, Omiki Shrine * Tsujido 4 shrines - Tai Shrine, Konpira Shrine, Benten Shrine, Gion Shrine * Hoshio 2 shrines - Inari Shrine, Mizu Shrine * Yamadahara 5 Shrines - Tenjin Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Hachioji Shrine, Myoken Shrine, Inari Shrine * Shimonakajima 5 shrines - Inari Shrine, Kifune Shrine, Gion Shrine, Toyokane Shrine, Akiba Shrine The following year, it was enshrined at Hoshio Ichinomiya Shrine (currently Gokasho Shrine, a precinct shrine). After the war, it belonged to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} First year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) New Year 27th, from the lower junior fifth rank to the upper junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Susagami.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Juichii ("{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}") - Written as "Susa Okami." === Shinto priest === Since the Middle Ages, our company's {{ill|lt=Shake|Q11588367|WD=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} has been held by the Oga clan (in olden times, they used the surnames Oe and Iwahashi). === Precincts === [[File:Susa-jinja (Arida) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Full view of the temple grounds. The approach is from left to right at the bottom of the image. On the upper right are the main shrine, oryoden, worship hall, and bell gate. ]]``The main hall'' is made of Ikkensha Sumikiri {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} and has a copper plate roof. The front pillar space is 1 ken, but the back and sides are 2 ken, and corner blocks are placed on the front pillars, which is a structure that follows the Kumano-zukuri style. . This was done by order of the feudal lord, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|Q319664|WD=Q319664|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}. (Japan)|Q1193848|Mashinori}} It was rebuilt in September of the first year ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}), and the Nakamura family, the head carpenters of the Kishu clan, was involved in its construction, and it appears that it was directly managed by the clan."Arita Regional Cultural Properties Catalog", Arita Regional Cultural Properties Protection Deliberation Committee Liaison Council, 1997. . On three sides of the front side of the main building, there is a rim with a raised railing (large floor), and side shoji screens are installed at the rear of both sides. The interior is divided into the {{ill|lt=inner hall|Q14616641|WD=Q14616641|1=Presbytery (architecture)}} and the outer hall by installing front and rear partitions on the central column of the main building, and a board door with a scroll, but it is said to be unusual in that the outer hall floor is one step higher than the kirikina nagaoshi and lower than the edge. Although there are few decorations such as carvings and the paintings are modest, it has a dignified appearance and is a valuable relic that shows the style of the Nakamura family . Originally it was {{ill|lt=cypress bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, but in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696} }), the roof was replaced with copper plates, and extensive repairs were carried out from {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|WD=Q2060|1=1992}}) to 9. This main shrine has been designated as a cultural property designated by Arita City.In front of the main shrine are the ``Chumon Gate'' and the ``Oratory Hall'', which are connected to the ``Oratory Hall''. The middle gate is a flat {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Karamon|1=UNKNOWN}}, the hall of worship is a flat {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with 3 girders and 2 beams, and the worship hall is a gabled tsumairi with 1 beam and 3 girders, and both have copper plate roofs. The hall of worship and the hall of worship are both open-air. The hall of worship is lower than the hall of worship, and the hall of worship has an upper floor in the center. The ``Suzumon'' (four-legged gate) standing in front of the shrine is {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} flat and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=tiled roof|1=UNKNOWN}}. In addition, there are shrine buildings such as Kama-dono, {{ill|lt=Kagura-den|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}, and Ema-do hall in the precincts. File:Susa-jinja (Arida) noritoden & honden.JPG|Main shrine (back right, city-designated cultural property) and hall of worship (front) File:Susa-jinja (Arida) suzumon.JPG|Suzumon File:Susa-jinja (Arida) emadou.JPG|Emadou File:Susa-jinja (Arida) torii.JPG|Torii in the precincts === Setsusha === [[File:Susa-jinja (Arida) keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Shinuchisha]] * Touhouden (Kotaijingu Shrine) - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukiyomison|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amateur Rikiojin|UNKNOWN|lt=Terikiojin}}. * West Hall (Izanami Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}}. * Gokasho Shrine and Inari Shrine (2 shrines {{ill|lt=Aiden|Q135009883|WD=Q135009883|1=Aidono}}) - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kikunsuchichi no Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} and 24 other shrines (Gokasho Shrine also enshrines the deities of the combined shrines at the end of the Meiji period). * Kanayama Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamabikojin|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. * Sumiyoshi Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Three Gods|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Omotesu Otoko no Mikoto, Nakazutsu Otoko no Mikoto, Sokotsutsu Otoko no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. * Itsukushima Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. * Shinkodani Shrine - Enshrined deity unknown. * Ita-Kiso Shrine Far Worship Hall * Minatogawa Shrine - Enshrined deity: Masashige Kusunoki. * Nanryusha - Enshrined deity: Yorinobu Tokugawa. * Konpira Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Oimono no Nushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Oomono no Nushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}. * Koyasu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tanatsuhime no Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}. === Festival === === Annual festival === * Monthly Festival (1st day of every month) *Monthly Festival (14th of every month) * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Congee fortune telling (January 15th) *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Lecture (February) * Spring Festival (Prayer Year Festival) (March) * Big catch festival (April) * Summer Purification (June) * Summer Festival Yoimiya (July 13th) * Summer Festival (July 14th) * Wakoshi (June 30th of the lunar calendar) * Senda Festival (October 14th) * Winter Festival (Ninamesai) (November) * Great purification ceremony (December 31st) === Main festivals === ; Senda Festival (regular festival) : {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}. Our {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} is called ``''Chida Festival''''. The annual festival used to be held on the first day of September {{ill|lt=rabbit|Q16662792|WD=Q16662792|lt=rabbit|1=mǎo}}. {{ill|lt=Masayasu|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}}4 ({{ill|lt=1647|Q6935|WD=Q6935|1=1647}}) Revised on September 14th by Yorinobu "Shrine Statement Book" in 1914. , later changed to {{ill|lt=solar calendar|Q210953|WD=Q210953|1=Solar calendar}} and became the current date.: The day before the festival (13th) is Yoimiya. The next day, on the 14th, after the Shinto ritual, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|lt=lion|1=Lion dance}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=haload|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=manager|Q7400093|WD=Q7400093|1=Saihai}} were performed in front of the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. alink|Forage|Q11498259}} A {{ill|lt=Lion Dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} consisting of {{ill|lt=Tengu|Q742360|WD=Q742360|1=Tengu}} and {{ill|lt=Oni|Q730052|WD=Q730052|1=Oni}} was dedicated. group="Note">The lion dance was previously held every other year by the two districts of Senda and Takada, but now it is held jointly ([http://bunkashisan.ne.jp/search/ViewContent.php?from=11&ContentID=241 Regional Cultural Asset Portal, Susa Shrine Lion Dance] Next, the mikoshi is carried to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho|1=UNKNOWN}} located to the southwest of the shrine.The mikoshi is thrown to the ground during the process.: A lion dance and other performances are held at the temple, and in the evening, a ``Tainage Shinji'' ceremony is held in which 6 {{ill|lt=tails#trivia|Q60960|WD=Q60960|lt=tails|1=Tail}} of sea bream are offered in front of the shrine and fishermen who have gathered compete for them. It is said that it was once so popular that people would say, ``If you want to see a bride, then it's the Senda Festival'' {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Corner)|1985}}, and even today it is also called the ``Senda Fighting Festival'' because fishermen fiercely compete for the sea bream thrown at them, believing that whoever gets it will be blessed with a big catch for a year. Afterwards, at midnight, they return to {{ill|lt=main shrine|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and the ritual ends. The festival mentioned above has been designated as a cultural property of Arita City as the ``Senda Festival.'' ; Congee fortune-telling ritual : {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}. {{ill|lt=Azuki bean porridge|Q115036294|WD=Q115036294|1=Azuki bean porridge}} is cooked in the cauldron in the temple grounds, and {{ill|lt=Glue fortune-telling|Q11604655|WD=Q11604655|1=Kayu ura}} is held to choose the best rice variety for the year from among 10 varieties. Afterwards, we eat red bean porridge and pray for good health throughout the year. According to ``Zoku Fudoki,'' until the Tensho period (the end of the 16th century), 12 sacred horses were dedicated from Itageso Shrine at the Senda Festival, and priests from the company participated in the ritual, and 12 priests from the company also visited the shrine every year on the first day of the New Year. It is thought that these were rituals in which the company's enshrined deity (Isotake no Mikoto) had an audience with his father deity {{Sfn|Susa Shrine (Gods; First Edition)|1986|p=350-352}}. === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * Tachi inscription: Inshu Jukagecho 1 mouth (with Itomaki Tachi Koshirae)*: It was dedicated in 1721, 50 years after the death of the founder of the Kii Tokugawa clan, {{ill|lt=Yorinobu|Q1045763|WD=Q1045763|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, by Yoshimune Tokugawa, Yorinobu's grandson and the eighth shogun. It was created by Kagenaga, a {{ill|lt=swordsmith|Q91012922|WD=Q91012922|1=Japanese swordsmith}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inshu|1=UNKNOWN}} (currently {{ill|lt=Tottori Prefecture|Q133935|WD=Q133935|1=Tottori Prefecture}}) during the Muromachi period. Blade length: 68.2cm, 1.8cm long, Kazukuri-an-bu. The figure of the sword is slender, the base iron is koitame, and the blade pattern is a thin straight blade (hoso-suguha), which is typical of Yamashiroden. ``Wakayama Prefecture Cultural Properties Guidebook'', Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education, 1999. . The Tachi Koshirae was made in the Edo period, and the thread wrapping and metal fittings are decorated with a warihishimon crest painted in shakudo iroe, and the {{ill|lt=sheath|Q661140|WD=Q661140|1=Scabbard}} is decorated with a warihishimon crest painted in {{ill|lt=maki-e|Q2083390|WD=Q2083390|1=Maki-e}}. {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}4 ({{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|WD=Q2084|1=1915}}) On March 26, 1915, National Treasure (so-called {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old National Treasure|1=UNKNOWN}}) It was designated as a {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} with the enactment of {{ill|lt=Cultural Properties Protection Law|Q1188502|WD=Q1188502|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law}} on August 29, 1950 ({{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|WD=Q18597|1=1950}}). === Cultural property designated by Arita City === * Main shrine * Tachi Mei Keishin 1 mouth (included Itomaki Tachi Koshirae) *: A sword made by Keishin Osafune, a resident of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}} (currently {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}) during the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. A tachi with a blade length of 63.6 cm and a length of 2.2 cm. The blade pattern is clove. Dedicated by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Munenao|Q6993442|WD=Q6993442|1=Tokugawa Munenao}}. The Koshirae was made in the Edo period, and both the {{ill|lt=handle|Q19508474|WD=Q19508474|1=grip}} and sheath are made of purple thread. The metal fittings show {{ill|lt=diamond|Q11620223|WD=Q11620223|1=Hishi mon}} painted in red bronze.The scabbard is decorated with {{ill|lt=Maki-e|Q2083390|WD=Q2083390|1=Maki-e}}, and the metal fittings and the Aoi crest (Aoi crest|Q11353976}}) are arranged alternately. * Senda Festival === Others === * Nanki Shigekuni dedication sword *: Although unspecified, this long sword has the inscription ``The petitioner is Kishu Jumonjusukan Sukejukuni 27 years old'' and has the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=god name|1=UNKNOWN}} of ``Susa Daimyojin'' engraved on it. The base and blade are thickly covered with rust, but {{jalink|Nanki Shigekuni #4th Generation It is known that it was dedicated on September 14, {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1690|Q7678|WD=Q7678|1=1690}}) by Shigekuni|Q11408354|4th Nanki Shigekuni}}, and from this dedication date and inscription, Shigekuni's year of birth can also be determined . * Motoori Norinaga poem monument *: In November of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 6th year ({{ill|lt=1794|Q7811|WD=Q7811|1=1794}}), {{ill|lt=Norinaga Motoori|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} read the {{ill|lt=adopted child|Q11572068|WD=Q11572068|1=yūshi}} At the invitation of the {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}} Iwahashi Tokizaki, he and I Ohira|Q3325272|Ohira}} stayed at the house of the priest, and preached {{ill|lt=old studies|Q9565755|WD=Q9565755|1=kogaku}} and {{ill|lt=poetry|Q11545302|WD=Q11545302|1=kadō}}. The monument was erected in November 1979 to commemorate his visit to our company on that occasion and the poem he wrote, ``This is the shrine of the gods of Iketaru, the kingdom of time, and the shrine of the gods of Fukaburo.'' Standing in front of the third torii gate. === Historical evidence === {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%; text-align:left" align="right" |+Similar internal company names of Kii and Izumo !God's name !{{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Kii Province|Q11604948|WD=Q11604948|lt=Kii|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province}} !{{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Izumo country|Q11395853|WD=Q11395853|lt=Izumo|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province}} |- !Itate | Date Shrine| />Sokinnoya Shrine Korea Itai Shrine |- !Kata |Kata Shrine||Kata Shrine |- !kumano ||Kumanoza Shrine||Kumanoza Shrine |- !Susa |Susa Shrine||Susa Shrine |- !Hayatama |Kumano Hayatama Shrine | | Hayatama Shrine |} [[File:Susa Shrine Hall of Worship.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[Sosa Shrine (Izumo City)|Susa Shrine]] ({{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}})]]Not only Susa Shrine, there are many shrines in {{ill|lt=Izumo|Q11395836|WD=Q11395836|1=Izumo}} and Kii that have the same company name (see the table on the right). Regarding this, there is a theory that both originated in Izumo and that the Izumo people brought their home country's religion with them when they settled in Kii. , there is a theory by Ken Matsumae and others that considers Kii to be the original homeland. Regarding Susanoo, it has been pointed out from an early stage that there is a discrepancy between the inner nature of Susanoo as a god of destruction and tyranny as seen in the Kiki and the inner nature as {{ill|lt=Bunka Hero|Q977690|WD=Q977690|lt=Bunka Hero God|1=Culture hero}} as seen in ``{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}'', and various theories have been proposed. .According to Ken Matsumae's theory, if Susanoo, which appears in both the Kojiki, Nihon Shoki, and Fudoki, is viewed as a separate entity, the deep kinship with the ocean-like and tree-like divinity and the land of roots seen in the Kojiki cannot be found in the Fudoki, but it can be seen in the Kii Company. From this, we infer that the Susanoo mentioned in the ``Kojiki'' and ``Nihonshoki'' refers to the deity enshrined at our company. He also cites the theory that the Land of Nenes, an ``eternal country'' in the Kii region where Susano-o is said to visit, was equated in the central government with Nekuni, which is the ``source of disaster'' and ``the land that sends back disasters,'' and that Susano-o was also seen as a god of destruction and tyranny that brought disaster. Furthermore, as supporting evidence that Susanoo from the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki is the deity enshrined by our company, we cite the importance placed on it by the Imperial Court, such as the designation of the shrine as a famous deity and records of investitures . Regarding the relationship with Izumo, Ken Matsumae also states that some of the Kaijin of Kii, who were honored with Susanoo and engaged in diplomacy with the continent (Korea), settled in Izumo. It is assumed that as the faith in Susanoo spread to the inland areas of the Izumo region, the inner nature of the ocean god etc. was lost. Another theory is that the complexity of Susanoo's divinity arose as a result of the thoughtless reunification of the Susanoo statues that existed in the central aristocracy and the Izumo region at the time of the compilation of the Kiki. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|2|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items
